title: tanacetum bipinnatum subsp. huronense
intro: Tanacetum bipinnatum subsp. huronense, also known as Huron Tansy, is a subspecies of the Tansy plant, characterized by its fern-like foliage and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties. The health benefits of Tanacetum bipinnatum subsp. huronense include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as a natural insect repellent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for various health applications, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
